response.justification: Strong engineering seniority (20 years) and full-stack technical breadth with staff-level responsibilities meet baseline expectations, but the role explicitly requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI.' The candidate's AI work is nascent (2 months at re:cinq on infrastructure) without evidence of shipped AI-powered products, and lacks the formal AI/ML education listed as preferred. Better suited for a general principal engineer role than AI strategy specifically.
strengths: 20 years of consistent engineering experience across full-stack, systems, and infrastructure domains
Staff-level seniority with demonstrated technical leadership and mentoring (workshops, teaching)
Currently engaged with modern AI infrastructure (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) showing directional awareness
concerns: Only 2 months of hands-on AI/infrastructure work; no demonstrated shipped products or platforms built with AI
No Master's degree or PhD in AI/ML (preferred qualification for principal-level AI strategy role)
Career trajectory emphasizes product engineering rather than strategic architecture or thought leadership in emerging technologies

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering seniority and breadth
